authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 141, Entry of Merchandise
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Homeland Security, U.S. Customs and Border Protection
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary U.S. customs-entry regulation for required entry information, invoice evidence, and import workflow prerequisites.

authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 142, Entry Process
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Homeland Security, U.S. Customs and Border Protection
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative CBP workflow rule for formal-entry process timing and progression through filing-adjacent states.

authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 143, Special Entry Procedures
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Homeland Security, U.S. Customs and Border Protection
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary CBP rule for informal and special-entry procedures that changes import-operations handling by shipment type and value.

authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 163, Recordkeeping
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Homeland Security, U.S. Customs and Border Protection
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary customs recordkeeping framework that governs what evidence must be preserved and produced to support import workflow closure.

authoritative_sources.title: Importing into the United States
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CBP's importer guide is the public operating reference for importer responsibilities, workflow stages, and operational import terminology.

authoritative_sources.title: ACE Cargo Release Implementation Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CBP's operational system reference for ACE cargo-release data requirements, message handling, and response semantics.

authoritative_sources.title: FinCEN Advisory FIN-2010-A001 on Trade-Based Money Laundering
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Financial Crimes Enforcement Network
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Treasury AML guidance for suspicious trade patterns such as over or under invoicing and unusual trade flows that can change whether import work may proceed.
used_for: suspicious value or document pattern handling

authoritative_sources.title: A Framework for OFAC Compliance Commitments
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Office of Foreign Assets Control
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary OFAC guidance for risk-based sanctions compliance, escalation, internal controls, and recordkeeping in international trade workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: WCO Data Model
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized customs data standard for harmonized data elements and consistent field-level evidence handling across customs systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Artifact and field normalization across systems

authoritative_sources.title: Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official U.S. tariff source for customs commodity terminology and classification-adjacent record normalization.

workflow_stages.name: Accepted intake and prerequisite verification
workflow_stages.description: Validate shipment references, importer-of-record context, broker authority, arrival status, commercial documents, and jurisdictional prerequisites before progression.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Entry-path selection and data normalization
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether the shipment follows standard or special-entry handling and normalize commodity, quantity, origin, and milestone data across source systems.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Filing coordination, release-status handling, and progression
workflow_stages.description: Move work through queued, filing-ready, filed, held, exam, released, delivery-pending, rework, or completed states based on ACE, broker-platform, and shipment evidence.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Exception, rework, and discrepancy management
workflow_stages.description: Handle missing documents, carrier or terminal mismatches, CBP or PGA holds, exam requests, and release discrepancies without silently skipping required process steps.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Fraud, AML, sanctions, and financial-impact escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when suspicious trade patterns, sanctions risks, or duty and payment impacts introduce regulated-risk or consumer-impact concerns outside the lane.

workflow_stages.name: Completion confirmation and evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Produce disposition and completion records only when release, reject-resolution, approved cancellation, or closure evidence is present and traceable.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

artifacts: Commercial invoice, packing list, bill of lading or airway bill, arrival notice, and broker instructions
Importer-of-record, consignee, bond, power-of-attorney, and shipment reference records
Entry, cargo-release, ABI, and ACE status artifacts including 3461 and 7501-adjacent data where applicable
Commodity descriptions, HTS references, origin data, valuation basis records, and PGA indicators
Carrier, terminal, FIRMS, freight-available, hold, exam, and delivery milestone records
Duty, fee, payment, statement, reconciliation, and communication-log artifacts
KYC, AML, sanctions, fraud, approval-history, and escalation records
systems_of_record: Customs broker operating systems and case queues
ERP, order management, and shipment source systems
General ledger, duty, and payment tracking systems
Sanctions, fraud, AML, and compliance monitoring systems

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, blocked, or out of scope based on import-execution workflow ownership.
Validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context required for safe shipment progression.
Prepare execution status updates, exception notes, trade execution summaries, and completion evidence records tied to named artifacts.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work based on documented lifecycle state and evidence.
escalate: Missing or contradictory shipment documents, release statuses, or tenant policies materially change the outcome.
Classification finality, valuation disputes, admissibility, or customs-law interpretation is required.
Fraud, AML, sanctions, or consumer-impact conditions change whether the work may safely proceed.
Disclosure, payment, or reconciliation conflicts affect duties, fees, or downstream customer impact.
refuse: Requests to fabricate approvals, regulated advice, broker authority, or completion evidence.
Requests that require legal, licensed, policy-override, or duty or payment approval authority outside the lane.
Requests to mark work complete while release, reject-resolution, cancellation, or closure evidence is missing.

domain_failure_modes: Progressing an import move without the minimum shipment, importer, or document truth set.
Treating unresolved hold, exam, or release mismatches as completed work.
Allowing ambiguous classification or valuation evidence to silently drive progression instead of escalation.
Ignoring TBML or sanctions indicators embedded in invoice, party, geography, or payment data.
Closing a case without traceable evidence of release, reject resolution, approved cancellation, or approved closure.
Applying public customs defaults when tenant policy requires stricter terminal, carrier, imaging, or approval controls.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant ownership split between import operations and delivery scheduling
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Some tenants keep drayage or final delivery dispatch inside import operations while others hand off at customs release.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling_rule: This pack constrains the lane to release-aware import execution and requires tenant routing rules before claiming delivery-disposition ownership.
